old_claim.note: Department Stores & Mass Retail provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Advertising agents and print campaigns are documented before the 1852 modern department-store milestone, so stores are later clients rather than origin prerequisites.

source_shape.source_locator: The Smithsonian traces newspaper advertising agents in the 1830s-1840s, urban billposting in the 1850s, and the later combination of industrialization, railroads, print media, and national markets.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Smithsonian chronology places newspaper-space agents in the 1830s and 1840s, before Le Bon Marche opened in 1852.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later retail customer can expand advertising demand without being the capability that created newspaper agents, handbills, or urban billposting.

ontology_before: advertising_mass_media_early inherited directly from department_stores_mass_retail as an origin or component dependency.
ontology_after: advertising_mass_media_early no longer inherits from department_stores_mass_retail; the source-pinned scope is represented by its remaining prerequisites.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: advertising_mass_media_early no longer has a direct prerequisite edge from department_stores_mass_retail.
would_reject_if: Direct historical evidence showed that department_stores_mass_retail was an operational prerequisite for the source-pinned advertising_mass_media_early milestone rather than later context or a parallel practice.
short_reason: Print advertising agencies predate the scoped modern department store.
